content.full_text:
State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Technical Assistance • • Administration • • Executive/Legal • • Enforcement
(916) 322-5662 322-5660 322-5901 322-6441

April 15, 1985

Honorable Willie L. Brown, Jr.
Speaker of the Assembly
State Capitol
Sacramento, CA 95814

Re: Advice No. 85-086

Dear Speaker Brown:

You have asked whether authoring a bill which codifies the
contents of an existing Executive Order which establishes an
Environmental Affairs Agency with budgetary and general policy
direction powers over the Solid Waste Management Board would
create a conflict of interest for a legislator/attorney who
represents a solid waste management company which has regulatory
relations with the Board. The bill does not in any way alter
the existing statutory or regulatory status of the Solid Waste
Management Board.

As you know, a legislator/attorney may have a conflict of
interest under the Political Reform Act if he authors a bill
which will have a material financial effect on one of his
clients. Government Code Sections 87100, 87103. In the
situation you describe, it does not appear foreseeable that the
bill creating the Environmental Affairs Agency will have any
financial effect on the legislator's client. Therefore, unless
the legislator knows of some special circumstances which
indicate that the bill is likely to directly affect his client,
there would be no conflict of interest. If there are any
additional facts which suggest that the creation of the agency
will affect the legislator's client, I would recommend that the
legislator write to the Commission's Legal Division for more
specific advice.

Very truly yours,

Barbara A. Milman
General Counsel

SACRAMENTO OFFICE
STATE CAPITOL
SACRAMENTO, CALIFORNIA 95814
(916) 445-8077

DISTRICT OFFICE
542 VAN NESS AVENUE
SAN FRANCISCO, CALIFORNIA 94102
(415) 557-2253

DISTRICT OFFICE
UN 301 - FEDERAL BUILDING
LOS ANGELES, CA 90012
213-688-5750

Assembly
California Legislature

APR 8 1995

WILLIE LEWIS BROWN, JR.
SPEAKER OF THE ASSEMBLY

April 4, 1985

Dan Stanford, Chairman
Fair Political Practices Commission
P. O. Box 807
Sacramento, California 95804

Dear Mr. Stanford:

Would authoring a bill which codifies the contents of an existing
Executive Order which establishes an Environmental Affairs Agency
with budgetary and general policy direction powers over the Solid
Waste Management Board create a conflict of interest for a
legislator/attorney who represents a solid waste management
company which has regulatory relations with the Board? The bill
does not in any way alter the existing statutory or regulatory
status of the Solid Waste Management Board.

I would appreciate your review of this matter and a response at
your earliest convenience.

Sincerely,

WILLIE L. BROWN, JR.
Speaker of the Assembly

WLB:ksd

sections.question: You have asked whether authoring a bill which codifies the contents of an existing Executive Order which establishes an Environmental Affairs Agency with budgetary and general policy direction powers over the Solid Waste Management Board would create a conflict of interest for a legislator/attorney who represents a solid waste management company which has regulatory relations with the Board. The bill does not in any way alter the existing statutory or regulatory status of the Solid Waste Management Board.
sections.conclusion: A legislator/attorney may have a conflict of interest under the Political Reform Act if he authors a bill which will have a material financial effect on one of his clients. Government Code Sections 87100, 87103. In the situation you describe, it does not appear foreseeable that the bill creating the Environmental Affairs Agency will have any financial effect on the legislator's client. Therefore, unless the legislator knows of some special circumstances which indicate that the bill is likely to directly affect his client, there would be no conflict of interest. If there are any additional facts which suggest that the creation of the agency will affect the legislator's client, the legislator should write to the Commission's Legal Division for more specific advice.
sections.question_synthetic: Whether a legislator/attorney who represents a solid waste management company would have a conflict of interest under the Political Reform Act by authoring a bill that codifies an Executive Order establishing an Environmental Affairs Agency with oversight powers over the Solid Waste Management Board.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: No conflict of interest exists under the Political Reform Act unless the bill would have a material financial effect on the legislator's client. Since the bill merely codifies an existing Executive Order without altering the statutory or regulatory status of the Board, no conflict appears foreseeable, unless special circumstances suggest the bill will directly affect the legislator's client.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document includes both the incoming request from Speaker Brown (dated April 4, 1985) and the FPPC response letter (dated April 15, 1985, Advice No. 85-086). Clear question and conclusion sections identified. Straightforward conflict of interest analysis.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: You have asked whether authoring a bill which codifies the contents of an existing Executive Order which establishes an Environmental Affairs Agency with budgetary and general policy direction powers over the Solid Waste Management Board would create a conflict of interest for a legislator/attorney who represents a solid waste management company which has regulatory relations with the Board. The bill does not in any way alter the existing statutory or regulatory status of the Solid Waste Management Board.

CONCLUSION: A legislator/attorney may have a conflict of interest under the Political Reform Act if he authors a bill which will have a material financial effect on one of his clients. Government Code Sections 87100, 87103. In the situation you describe, it does not appear foreseeable that the bill creating the Environmental Affairs Agency will have any financial effect on the legislator's client. Therefore, unless the legislator knows of some special circumstances which indicate that the bill is likely to directly affect his client, there would be no conflict of interest. If there are any additional facts which suggest that the creation of the agency will affect the legislator's client, the legislator should write to the Commission's Legal Division for more specific advice.

embedding.summary: FPPC advice letter in response to Speaker Willie Brown's inquiry regarding whether a legislator/attorney could have a conflict of interest by authoring a bill codifying an Executive Order establishing an Environmental Affairs Agency with oversight of the Solid Waste Management Board, where the legislator represents a company regulated by the Board. The FPPC concluded no conflict exists unless the bill would have a material financial effect on the legislator's client.
